mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiple expansion, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, the market discounts tighter financial conditions and weaker earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the sign is not negative after conditioning on inflation above 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and softer future demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if a 3-month increase in UNRATE fails to precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if rolling 3-year IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS expands while UNRATE is flat or falling, the labor market is adding jobs without material slack, supporting consumption and risk appetite and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE change over same window <= 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates weakening retail demand and slower nominal activity, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days as revenue growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the sign flips in rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals cooling housing demand and weaker cyclical growth, which should hurt equities over the next 60 trading days through lower earnings expectations and reduced risk appetite.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contractions below -5% are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the sign is not stable across subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving supply capacity and disinflation, which should support equity margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined condition does not beat either proxy alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if triggered periods fail to produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth surprise in GDPC1 relative to the prior quarter signals stronger aggregate demand and earnings growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 changes are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of observations, or if the predictive sign is absent in any 3-year rolling window.
